patterns.name: Memory firings log + decay analysis with bootstrap gate
patterns.summary: Append one line per memory-that-fired to a session-indexed log; decay analysis surfaces dormant memories; analysis gated on 10 distinct sessions to avoid false signal during bootstrap. Concrete cost: one append per turn.
patterns.applies_when: JASON-OS auto-memory exceeds ~30-40 entries OR stale-memory drift becomes visible

patterns.name: Two-tier user-readable vs machinery file separation
patterns.summary: User-facing daily-read files at the top level (Command Center, To-Do); machinery (session counter, hot context, append-only logs) lives behind a single folder boundary (_system/). The split is the design.
patterns.applies_when: JASON-OS grows a user-facing layer beyond CLAUDE.md and SESSION_CONTEXT.md

patterns.name: /audit skill as scheduled aggregate cross-system check
patterns.summary: Read-mostly periodic check (every 7 sessions). Six dimensions: redundant memory, stale memory, MEMORY.md index drift, broken wikilinks, skill-vs-system drift, gated decay analysis. Surfaces findings; user picks fixes.
patterns.applies_when: JASON-OS skill count or rule count grows past ~20 and aggregate drift becomes invisible

patterns.name: Setup skill as runnable first-time interview
patterns.summary: Skippable conversational interview that ships a working system in 10 minutes; minimum 4 questions then explicit ship-it-or-keep-going gate. Edge cases for input variations handled inline.
patterns.applies_when: JASON-OS ships as a clone-and-run template (not a current goal)

patterns.name: Use-both / bridge-via-Claude-MCP architecture pattern
patterns.summary: Personal layer in tool A + collaborative layer in tool B + Claude reading both via MCP. Maps onto SoNash + JASON-OS + cross-repo-movement-reframe split. Shape reference, not a port.
patterns.applies_when: Cross-repo-movement-reframe planning when the layer split is up for design

patterns.name: ADAM coherence_monitor.py reading session JSONL for compaction events
patterns.summary: Read Claude Code JSONL session files; detect compaction events; write alerts. JASON-OS has compact-restore.js as part of session-start hook layer. Pattern is to MONITOR session JSONL for state changes.
patterns.applies_when: When JASON-OS hook-layer expansion is on the agenda. Worth a future /repo-analysis on the ADAM repo itself.

patterns.name: Dual-mode docs (human-readable AND LLM-walkthrough-ready)
patterns.summary: README written so a human reads it as install steps OR pastes it into Claude and Claude walks them through. obsidian-setup.md has 'For the human reading this' section followed by 'Below the line is Claude's operating manual.'

knowledge.name: Rubber-stamp danger for AI-drafted feedback memories
knowledge.summary: When the model drafts a feedback memory after a correction, the same model that just made the mistake is now writing the rule meant to prevent it. Review like an intern wrote it; reject anything that sounds self-exculpating; tighten rules that try to cover more ground than the moment justified.
knowledge.where_to_apply: JASON-OS auto-memory writing instructions in CLAUDE.md (or a tenet memory)

knowledge.name: Search-vs-read structural design tenet
knowledge.summary: AI working through a search-API-mediated workspace degrades on cross-source synthesis; AI with direct file-system access handles it. Articulates the access-pattern angle of JASON-OS's existing filesystem-verification posture.
knowledge.where_to_apply: Pair with tenet_filesystem_verification.md as a tenet candidate, or extend that tenet with the access-pattern angle

knowledge.name: Tool-cost ranking + cheapest-tool-first rule
knowledge.summary: Ranking: raw file read < CLI shell exec < eval-via-CLI < MCP server. MCP servers cost tokens before any call (schemas load up-front). Rule: 'raw reads for known paths, CLI for search and listings, semantic only for cross-domain, MCP only when you need rendered view.' JASON-OS has ToolSearch for deferred MCP tools but no stated principle.
knowledge.where_to_apply: JASON-OS tenet candidate. Authors picking among Read/Bash/Grep/Skill/MCP every day get a rule of thumb.

knowledge.name: Format-longevity / plain-text-outlives-the-tool
knowledge.summary: Plain-text markdown is readable in any text editor in 30 years; proprietary databases aren't. Asymmetric migration pain (Notion→Obsidian clean, Obsidian→Notion lossy). JASON-OS lives this implicitly; doesn't articulate it.
knowledge.where_to_apply: Tenet candidate. Worth surfacing if/when a substrate decision is up for debate.

knowledge.name: Compact four-laws crystallization as CLAUDE.md frame
knowledge.summary: Four operating laws (Operating Constraint, Verification Protocol, Scope Discipline, Execute-Don't-Meta-Talk) with rationale and copy-pasteable instruction blocks. Compactness contrasts with JASON-OS CLAUDE.md §4's 16 behavioral guardrails — comparison data for a future trim pass.

knowledge.name: MEMORY.md index discipline: specific descriptions are load-bearing
knowledge.summary: MEMORY.md is loaded every session; specific descriptions let the model route, generic ones don't. JASON-OS auto-memory MEMORY.md already follows this convention.
knowledge.where_to_apply: Already in place — validates current JASON-OS pattern.

knowledge.name: Memory has decay; needs scheduled review with bootstrap gate
knowledge.summary: Memory rules go stale. Two mechanisms: ambient (when a memory fires and the user disagrees, the rule gets revisited) + scheduled (every N sessions, surface dormant memories). Bootstrap gate prevents false-positive dormant flags during initial accumulation.

knowledge.name: Don't-batch-install operational discipline
knowledge.summary: 'Add these one at a time. Verify each one works before adding the next. Never batch-install MCP servers and hope they all come up.' Generalizes to skill-porting, hook-adding, dependency-introduction.
knowledge.where_to_apply: JASON-OS conventions or CLAUDE.md §4 as a stated cross-cutting rule

knowledge.name: CREDITS.md as a maintained attribution protocol
knowledge.summary: Written rules for when attribution gets added; per-file docstring requirements for code-adapted vs concept-adapted; full MIT copyright + URL + path on code-adapted. JASON-OS has SoNash lineage that the whole repo embodies.
knowledge.where_to_apply: When JASON-OS ↔ SoNash cross-pollination gets dense enough that informal attribution stops working

knowledge.name: Adjacent-implementations listing as research-honesty discipline
knowledge.summary: Naming peer projects (with rationale per project) so 'the field is named rather than strawmanned.' JASON-OS deep-research outputs already cite sources; a discrete 'adjacent implementations' framing for any public-facing positioning would borrow this discipline.
knowledge.where_to_apply: Inspirational shape for future research-output framing

knowledge.name: Markdown-as-substrate scaling pattern (Dataview/Smart-Connections/Templater inspiration)
knowledge.summary: Obsidian plugin ecosystem demonstrates that typed-markdown corpus with frontmatter has more depth than 'a folder of notes': declarative queries, local-embedding semantic layer, logic-in-templates. Existence proof of where markdown-substrate systems can grow.
knowledge.where_to_apply: When JASON-OS auto-memory or .research/ output grows to need cross-source synthesis beyond keyword grep

knowledge.name: Editor-as-CLI tool-tier insight
knowledge.summary: Author's hard-won lesson: 'I spent weeks doing MCP gymnastics that the CLI would have replaced in a single command.' Validates the cheapest-tool-first principle (K-03) from a different angle.

knowledge.name: Rule-why-apply format for feedback memories
knowledge.summary: JASON-OS auto-memory rules already use this format. Validates the convention.

anti_patterns_for_creator_view.name: Don't ship a public template with no CI security pipeline
anti_patterns_for_creator_view.summary: ai-chief-of-staff ships executable Python in a public MIT-licensed template with no Gitleaks, no Dependabot, no Semgrep. JASON-OS already has the security pipeline; preserve that posture.
anti_patterns_for_creator_view.lesson: Narrow threat surface excuses many absences; missing secrets-scanning isn't one of them for any public repo with executable code.

anti_patterns_for_creator_view.name: Don't auto-fix during decay analysis
anti_patterns_for_creator_view.summary: ai-chief-of-staff /audit is read-mostly: surfaces findings; user picks fixes. Memory deletion or merging without explicit human approval is a known anti-pattern.
anti_patterns_for_creator_view.lesson: When porting decay analysis, preserve the surface-don't-delete discipline.

anti_patterns_for_creator_view.name: Don't conflate single-operator simplicity with skill-system simplicity
anti_patterns_for_creator_view.summary: ai-chief-of-staff has 5 skills because it's one person's daily loop. JASON-OS solves a different problem where one-person-loop simplicity isn't the target.
anti_patterns_for_creator_view.lesson: Compactness inspires; it doesn't prescribe. Don't trim JASON-OS skill count to match.

anti_patterns_for_creator_view.name: Don't dismiss artifacts because the technology is unfamiliar
anti_patterns_for_creator_view.summary: Meta-lesson from doing this analysis. The Obsidian-specific docs were the most productive single source of JASON-OS-relevant ideas in the entire repo. Specifics inspire by analogy.
anti_patterns_for_creator_view.lesson: Coverage Audit reads-by-default. Relevance is post-read judgment. SKILL.md v1.1 hard-gates this.

anti_patterns_for_creator_view.name: Don't reach for the heaviest tool first
anti_patterns_for_creator_view.summary: Author: 'I spent weeks doing MCP gymnastics that the CLI would have replaced in a single command.'
anti_patterns_for_creator_view.lesson: Reach for the cheapest tool that does the job. JASON-OS skill authors who default to MCP server calls when Read or Grep would do are paying the cost up-front.

schema_drift_note: JASON-OS analysisRecord schema (scripts/lib/analysis-schema.js) prunes 'anti-pattern' and 'content' candidate types per PORT_DECISIONS.md Batch 2. SKILL.md still describes 4 candidate types in Phase 6 prose. Drift logged as a finding for future reconciliation; this value-map.json keeps anti-patterns in a separate non-schema field for Creator View §6 reference.
